critical.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
critical.reality: The 22-check suite in scripts/smoke_test.py is a black-box HTTP smoke test requiring a live running server (--url flag, cookie-based auth). It is not an E2E integration test of the internal build pipeline. It tests API surface reachability, not the session→evaluator→push pipeline phases. No pytest-level integration tests for sessions, evaluator, or push exist in the provided test files.
critical.recommendation: Create pytest integration tests under tests/test_pipeline_e2e.py that mock claude_agent_sdk and test the full session lifecycle (build → eval → push) without requiring a live server.

critical.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
critical.reality: The only pytest files provided (test_auth.py, test_webhook.py) are pure unit tests covering auth and webhook signature verification only. There are zero integration tests covering sessions.py, the evaluator phase (_run_single_eval), or _phase_push(). The CLAUDE.md itself admits test_sdk_phase2.py has 3 pre-existing failures because claude_agent_sdk is not installed locally, meaning SDK-path tests never pass in the standard test run.
critical.recommendation: Add integration tests that mock claude_agent_sdk.query() (per the AsyncMock pattern documented in CLAUDE.md) and exercise session creation, evaluator invocation, and push phase in a single pytest fixture.

high.claim: spec claims Linear two-way sync — webhook triggers builds AND state updates flow back to Linear
high.reality: webhook.py only handles inbound Linear webhook parsing and HMAC verification. There are no outbound Linear API calls in any provided file — no issueUpdate GraphQL mutation, no linear_update_issue helper, no HTTP client call to api.linear.app from Python. The sync is strictly one-directional: Linear → Pi-CEO.
high.recommendation: Implement an outbound Linear state-update helper (e.g. POST api.linear.app/graphql with issueUpdate mutation) and call it from routes/webhooks.py after a session reaches a terminal state to close the loop.

high.reality: The provided source file (dashboard/app/api/telegram/route.ts) contains helper functions (triggerBuild, getPiCeoStatus, getLinearBacklog) but is truncated before the POST request handler where command routing (/start, /help, /build dispatch) would live. No explicit /start or /help command handlers are visible in the code — only infrastructure functions that could back a /build and /status command.
high.recommendation: Read the full file to verify the POST handler includes a command-dispatch block (e.g. text.startsWith('/start'), text.startsWith('/help'), text.startsWith('/build')); if absent, add explicit handlers for /start (welcome + usage), /help (list commands), and /build <repo> <brief> (call triggerBuild).

high.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
high.reality: scripts/smoke_test.py uses urllib directly against a live BASE URL with cookie-based auth — it cannot run in CI without a pre-deployed server. The smoke-prod CI job (per CLAUDE.md) only runs on main-branch and requires TAO_PROD_PASSWORD secret, meaning the 22-check suite is never exercised on PRs and does not gate the build pipeline at merge time.
high.recommendation: Gate the smoke test on every PR by adding a CI job that spins up the FastAPI server in-process using httpx.AsyncClient(app=app, base_url='http://test') rather than hitting a live URL.

high.claim: Budget tracker is instantiated but not wired — BudgetTracker in src/tao/budget/tracker.py is a correct implementation, but session_sdk.py does not instantiate it
high.reality: The spec itself documents this gap as an unresolved known issue (not ✅ Complete), yet no test exists to enforce that budget tracking is eventually wired — there is no failing test that would catch a regression or confirm when the wiring is done.
high.recommendation: Add a test in tests/test_budget_tracker.py that imports session_sdk and asserts BudgetTracker.tick() is called with a non-zero cost_delta after a mocked SDK invocation, so the gap becomes a failing test rather than a comment.

low.reality: The HISTORY map is in-process memory (const HISTORY = new Map). On Vercel serverless, each cold start gets a fresh Map, so multi-turn conversation context is silently lost between requests — the spec implies a functional conversational interface but the implementation has no persistent storage.
low.recommendation: Persist conversation history to an external store (Edge Config, Upstash Redis, or Supabase) keyed by chatId, or document that stateless single-turn mode is intentional and strip the history logic.

low.claim: op:// refs in .env not resolved by dotenv — Pydantic validator must detect and return None
low.reality: No test in the provided test files validates the Pydantic field_validator that detects op:// strings. The pattern is documented in CLAUDE.md as a hardwired lesson but no regression test exists to prevent it being silently removed.
low.recommendation: Add a test in tests/test_config.py that constructs a Settings/config object with a field value of 'op://vault/item/field' and asserts the validated value is None.
